steps.title: Track the contrast
steps.content: The text first presents specialization as useful: the Kaua'i 'amakihi has physical and behavioral traits that help it survive in a specific habitat. The word However then turns that same specialization into a vulnerability because environmental changes can disrupt the ecosystem it depends on.

steps.title: Keep both main points
steps.content: The main idea must include both sides of that trade-off: the bird is unique to the Hawaiian archipelago and highly specialized, and that specialization makes it especially susceptible to habitat disturbance. A adds unsupported habitat-sharing information, B omits the vulnerability, and D overgeneralizes to Hawaiian birds broadly. The answer is C.
